heroHeadline: Russia Launches Largest Aerial Attack in Months: 666 Drones and Missiles Batter Ukraine — RAF Shoots Down Russian Drone in NATO First
heroSubtitle: Russia deployed 666 aerial assets overnight — 619 drones and 47 missiles — against Ukraine on April 25, one of the largest combined strikes since 2022. Dnipro endured 12+ consecutive hours of attacks: 5 killed, 30+ injured, a four-story building destroyed. Chernihiv Oblast's Nizhyn recorded 2 killed. Romanian NATO territory was impacted by drone debris for the first time, prompting Bucharest to summon the Russian ambassador. In a historic NATO first, a Royal Air Force Eurofighter Typhoon shot down a Russian drone approximately 1.5 km from the Romanian-Ukrainian border — the first confirmed kill of a Russian UAV by a NATO aircraft. Zelensky traveled to Azerbaijan and offered to hold trilateral Ukraine-Russia-US peace talks there; Moscow has not responded.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OHCHR, ISW, CSIS, Ukrainian MoD, US DoD, NATO, IAEA, EU Council, UK MoD, and major news outlets including Reuters, BBC, AP, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Kyiv Independent, Euronews, and RBC-Ukraine. Casualty figures are estimates; Russia has never released official figures.
